metadata.mission: Add allowlist layering, compose extensibility, streaming exec, and destroy extraction as generic platform primitives while dropping CI-specific product scope.
assumptions: All new parameters default to None/False, preserving existing behavior when not explicitly used
compose_extras and best_effort=False are internal-only APIs, not CLI-exposed
PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH is operator-trusted input; the host operator controls the environment

metadata.description: Implement the core allowlist layering mechanism in unified-proxy config loading. This is the security-critical foundation that all other extensibility builds on.

Add a pure function merge_allowlist_configs(base: AllowlistConfig, extra: AllowlistConfig) -> AllowlistConfig that performs additive-only merge. No side effects, no env var awareness. Merge semantics: domains with ordered dedup (base first, extra appended, exact string match); HTTP endpoints same-host union methods (uppercased dedup) and paths (exact dedup); HTTP endpoints new-host append; blocked paths append (no per-host dedup). Merged result uses base config version. Output is fully determined by input ordering — no sorting or normalization beyond dedup rules.
acceptance_criteria: Function signature: merge_allowlist_configs(base: AllowlistConfig, extra: AllowlistConfig) -> AllowlistConfig
Domains merged with ordered dedup (base entries first, extra appended)
Same-host HTTP endpoints: methods uppercased and deduped, paths deduped
New-host HTTP endpoints: appended preserving extra ordering
Blocked paths: extra appended after base (no per-host dedup)
Merged result uses base config version
Pure function — no side effects, no env var reads

metadata.description: Add extra_path: str | None = None parameter to load_allowlist_config(). Precedence: explicit extra_path arg > PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH env var. When extra_path is None, resolve via env var using same indirection pattern as get_config_path() (extend it or add parallel helper — do not inline os.environ.get). When extra_path is explicitly passed (even empty string), env var is not consulted. When extra path resolves to unset/empty, skip extra loading (no behavior change). When extra path is set but file missing, raise ConfigError (fail-closed). If extra loading or validation fails, error propagates — proxy will not start with partially-configured allowlist.
acceptance_criteria: New parameter: extra_path: str | None = None on load_allowlist_config()
Explicit extra_path arg takes priority over PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH env var
When extra_path is None, falls back to env var via get_config_path() pattern
When extra path is unset/empty, skips extra loading (current behavior preserved)
When extra path points to missing file, raises ConfigError (fail-closed)
Extra loading/validation errors propagate — no partial allowlist startup

metadata.description: Extra allowlist files may omit top-level keys non-empty in the base (e.g., extra with only http_endpoints is valid). version field is still required; validated (must be non-empty) but value ignored during merge. When domains is omitted, treat as empty list for merge purposes. Relaxation happens at parsing stage (before constructing AllowlistConfig); AllowlistConfig.__post_init__ validation only applies to merged result. Do not add flags or subclasses to AllowlistConfig to support partial construction.
acceptance_criteria: Extra file may omit domains key (treated as empty list for merge)
Extra file may omit http_endpoints key (treated as empty list)
Extra file may omit blocked_paths key (treated as empty list)
version field is still required in extra file
Extra file version value is ignored during merge (base version used)
AllowlistConfig.__post_init__ validation applied only to merged result, not partial extra
No flags or subclasses added to AllowlistConfig

metadata.description: Comprehensive test suite for allowlist merge logic. Tests: valid merge produces expected combined config; missing extra file raises ConfigError; invalid extra schema raises ConfigError; domain dedup preserves base ordering; duplicate domain in extra not repeated; same-host endpoint union merges methods and paths; blocked path append ordering; blocked paths with same host in both base and extra are appended (not merged per-host); extra file with only http_endpoints (omitted domains) is valid; version mismatch uses base version; precedence: explicit extra_path overrides env var; precedence: env var fallback when extra_path is None; precedence: explicit empty string extra_path means no extra.
acceptance_criteria: Test: valid merge produces expected combined config
Test: missing extra file raises ConfigError
Test: invalid extra schema raises ConfigError
Test: domain dedup preserves base ordering
Test: duplicate domain in extra not repeated in merged result
Test: same-host endpoint union merges methods and paths
Test: blocked path append ordering
Test: same-host blocked paths in both base and extra are appended
Test: extra file with only http_endpoints (omitted domains) is valid
Test: version mismatch — merged result uses base version
Test: explicit extra_path arg overrides env var
Test: env var fallback when extra_path is None
Test: explicit empty string extra_path means no extra

metadata.description: Write ADR covering additive-only merge design and rationale. Document merge canonicalization rules (exact string match for domains and hosts, uppercase dedup for methods, ordered dedup for domains, append-only for blocked paths). Document precedence (explicit extra_path arg > env var; container env always uses container mount path). Document fail-closed failure policy (configured extra that is missing/invalid prevents startup). Follow existing ADR template (ADR-000 through ADR-007 exist).
acceptance_criteria: ADR follows existing template structure (Status, Context, Decision, Consequences)
Documents additive-only merge design and security rationale
Documents merge canonicalization rules (string match, uppercase methods, ordered dedup)
Documents precedence rules (arg > env var, container mount path)
Documents fail-closed failure policy

metadata.description: Verify all Phase 1 tasks are implemented correctly. All merge tests pass. load_allowlist_config() with no extra path produces identical behavior to current code. ADR-008 is complete and follows template.
Add compose extensibility, streaming exec, destroy extraction, and container environment plumbing. These are independent runtime primitives that improve automation ergonomics.

task-2-1.title: Add compose_extras parameter to compose functions

metadata.description: Add compose_extras: list[str] | None = None to get_compose_command(), compose_up(), and compose_down(). Each entry is a path to a compose override file. Validated: each path must exist on disk and be a regular file (not directory or device); raise FileNotFoundError if not (TOCTOU acceptable for CLI). File ordering in -f chain: base (docker-compose.yml) → credential-isolation (if enabled) → override_file (if set) → compose_extras (in list order). None or empty list preserves current behavior exactly. This is an internal Python API (not CLI-exposed). Callers responsible for passing same extras to compose_down() if extras define new services; not required for extras that only add mounts/env to existing services.
acceptance_criteria: New parameter compose_extras: list[str] | None = None on get_compose_command()
New parameter compose_extras on compose_up() and compose_down()
Each extras path validated: must exist and be a regular file
FileNotFoundError raised for invalid/missing paths
File ordering: base → credential-isolation → override_file → compose_extras
None or empty list produces identical command to current behavior

metadata.description: Add exec_in_container_streaming(container_id: str, *args: str, timeout: int = 3600) -> int. Uses subprocess.Popen with inherited stdout/stderr (real-time streaming). Returns process exit code on normal completion. Timeout sequence: proc.terminate() (SIGTERM) → proc.wait(timeout=5) → on TimeoutExpired: proc.kill() (SIGKILL) + proc.wait() to reap. Best-effort docker stop --time 10 backstop on timeout (intentionally destructive — appropriate for ephemeral/automation use cases). Log warning with container ID on backstop failure. Return exit code 124 on timeout (coreutils convention). Does not replace existing exec_in_container().
acceptance_criteria: Function signature: exec_in_container_streaming(container_id, *args, timeout=3600) -> int
Uses subprocess.Popen with inherited stdout/stderr
Returns process exit code on normal completion
On timeout: SIGTERM → wait(5s) → SIGKILL → reap
On timeout: best-effort docker stop --time 10 backstop
Returns exit code 124 on timeout
Existing exec_in_container() unchanged

metadata.description: Extract destroy_impl(name: str, keep_worktree: bool = False, best_effort: bool = True, skip_tmux: bool = False, skip_branch_cleanup: bool = False) -> None. Uses best_effort (not force) for error-handling semantics. Default best_effort=True preserves current cast destroy behavior (tolerates partial state). Click wrapper maps: --force → skip confirmation AND best_effort=True; --yes and SANDBOX_NONINTERACTIVE only skip confirmation (do NOT imply best_effort). Keep existing log_info/log_warn calls inside destroy_impl; move remaining click.echo calls to Click wrapper. destroy_impl does not prompt. best_effort=False (strict mode): each cleanup step that currently catches/logs errors will re-raise instead. On first exception, cleanup stops. Strict mode is internal-only (not CLI-exposed). Gate tmux kill on skip_tmux, branch cleanup on skip_branch_cleanup. Preserve all existing cleanup steps and ordering.
acceptance_criteria: Function signature: destroy_impl(name, keep_worktree=False, best_effort=True, skip_tmux=False, skip_branch_cleanup=False)
Default best_effort=True preserves current behavior
Click wrapper maps --force to skip_confirm + best_effort=True
Click wrapper maps --yes/SANDBOX_NONINTERACTIVE to skip_confirm only
log_info/log_warn calls remain inside destroy_impl
click.echo calls moved to Click wrapper
destroy_impl does not prompt
best_effort=False re-raises on first cleanup failure
skip_tmux=True skips tmux kill
skip_branch_cleanup=True skips branch cleanup
All existing cleanup steps and ordering preserved

metadata.description: In compose_up(): if host env var PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH is set, resolve to absolute path, validate exists (FileNotFoundError), validate is regular file, resolve symlinks via os.path.realpath(). Bind-mount the extra file read-only into container at /etc/unified-proxy/allowlist-extra.yml. Set PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH=/etc/unified-proxy/allowlist-extra.yml in container env. Inject via generated temp compose override file (tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False)), appended through compose_extras. Clean up temp file in try/finally (SIGKILL leaks acceptable). If host env var unset/empty, no mount or env var added. If isolate_credentials=False (no proxy), silently ignore the env var. Trust model: operator-trusted input, mounted read-only.
acceptance_criteria: When PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH is set: resolves to absolute path
Validates file exists and is a regular file
Resolves symlinks via os.path.realpath()
Bind-mounts read-only to /etc/unified-proxy/allowlist-extra.yml
Sets container env PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH to container mount path
Injects via temp compose override file through compose_extras
Temp file cleaned up in try/finally
Unset/empty env var: no mount or env var added
isolate_credentials=False: env var silently ignored

metadata.description: Test compose_extras parameter: correct -f ordering with all layers active (base + credential-isolation + override + extras); None preserves current behavior; invalid path raises FileNotFoundError; full 4-layer ordering verified; compose_down succeeds without allowlist extra override file present (lifecycle stability).
acceptance_criteria: Test: compose_extras appends -f args in correct order
Test: full 4-layer ordering (base + credential-isolation + override + extras)
Test: compose_extras=None produces same command as current
Test: invalid extras path raises FileNotFoundError
Test: compose_down succeeds without allowlist extra override file present

metadata.description: Test exec_in_container_streaming: normal completion returns exit code; timeout returns 124; timeout triggers SIGTERM then SIGKILL sequence; non-existent container returns docker exec exit code (immediate failure).
acceptance_criteria: Test: normal completion returns process exit code
Test: timeout returns exit code 124
Test: timeout triggers SIGTERM → wait → SIGKILL sequence
Test: non-existent container returns docker exec exit code

metadata.description: Test destroy_impl: skip_tmux=True skips tmux kill; skip_branch_cleanup=True skips branch cleanup; default best_effort=True logs and skips missing resources; explicit best_effort=False raises on missing resources; logging output verification (capture log_warn/log_info calls).
acceptance_criteria: Test: skip_tmux=True skips tmux kill step
Test: skip_branch_cleanup=True skips branch cleanup
Test: default best_effort=True logs and skips missing resources
Test: explicit best_effort=False raises on first missing resource
Test: logging output verification (log_warn/log_info captured)

metadata.description: Test container env plumbing: PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH set generates override with read-only mount; unset produces no extra mount or env var; pointing to nonexistent file raises FileNotFoundError; temp override file cleaned up on success; temp override file cleaned up on failure (exception path).
acceptance_criteria: Test: PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH set generates override with read-only mount
Test: PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH unset produces no extra mount/env
Test: nonexistent file path raises FileNotFoundError
Test: temp override file cleaned up after compose_up succeeds
Test: temp override file cleaned up after compose_up fails

Ensure no regressions, update docs for the retained generic features, and finalize the ADR.

journal.title: Task Completed: Write ADR-008: Allowlist Layering
journal.content: Created docs/adr/008-allowlist-layering.md following the existing ADR template. Documents: additive-only merge design with security rationale; merge canonicalization rules (exact string match for domains/hosts, uppercase dedup for methods, ordered dedup, append-only for blocked paths); precedence rules (explicit arg > env var > no extra); extra file schema relaxation; fail-closed failure policy. Includes Alternatives Considered section explaining rejection of override-based merge, deep merge with per-host dedup, and AllowlistConfig subclass approach.

journal.content: Threaded PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH into container in foundry_sandbox/docker.py compose_up(). When isolate_credentials=True and host env var is set: resolves to absolute path via os.path.realpath(), validates exists and is regular file (FileNotFoundError), generates temp compose override YAML that bind-mounts read-only to /etc/unified-proxy/allowlist-extra.yml and sets PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH env var in container, appends temp file to compose_extras list.
